summary: Italian-American composer and pianist (1924–1994), best known for film and television music. Wrote 'Moon River' (lyrics by Johnny Mercer) for Audrey Hepburn in Breakfast at Tiffany's and the 'Pink Panther Theme.' Also composed the Peter Gunn soundtrack and 'Days of Wine and Roses.' His piano arrangement of Nino Rota's Romeo and Juliet love theme knocked the Beatles off the #1 spot on Billboard.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Italian-American film and television composer and pianist. His piano arrangement of Nino Rota's love theme from Franco Zeffirelli's Romeo and Juliet (1968) succeeded the Beatles' 'Get Back' as Billboard's #1 song. Composed several iconic soundtracks for director Blake Edwards (Pink Panther series, Breakfast at Tiffany's). Wikipedia

works.name: "Moon River"
works.description: Song written for Audrey Hepburn's character Holly Golightly in Breakfast at Tiffany's (1961). Lyrics by Johnny Mercer, who describes a 'huckleberry friend' and the river as 'wider than a mile.' In 3/4 time; melody opens with 'long C, short high G, F, [pause] E, eighth notes D-C-B-flat, long C.' In Truman Capote's original novella, Holly was supposed to abscond to Brazil, but the film's ending with 'Moon River' was used instead. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Written for Audrey Hepburn in Breakfast at Tiffany's; lyrics by Johnny Mercer ('huckleberry friend,' 'wider than a mile')
examples: Johnny Mercer's lyrics describe a 'huckleberry friend' as 'wider than a mile' in a song this composer wrote for Audrey Hepburn.
An ending by this author [Truman Capote] in which a woman sets a cat free and absconds to Brazil was altered for an adaptation that uses Henry Mancini's 'Moon River.'

works.name: "The Pink Panther Theme"
works.description: Iconic jazz theme for the 1963 film The Pink Panther (directed by Blake Edwards). Famous for the bass line that chromatically ascends from C-sharp to a long E in swung eighth notes, setting up the saxophone melody. Wikipedia

works.name: "Days of Wine and Roses"
works.description: Song by Mancini for the 1962 film of the same name. Lyrics inspired by an Ernest Dowson poem; the lyrics consist of two run-on sentences.

comprehensive_summary: Henry Mancini (1924–1994) was an Italian-American film and television composer and pianist. He is best known for 'Moon River,' written for Audrey Hepburn's role as Holly Golightly in Breakfast at Tiffany's (1961), with lyrics by Johnny Mercer ('huckleberry friend,' 'wider than a mile'). The song is in 3/4 time and opens with the melody 'long C, short high G, F, [pause] E, eighth notes D-C-B-flat, long C.' In Truman Capote's original novella, Holly was supposed to escape to Brazil, but the film's ending using 'Moon River' was substituted. Mancini's other iconic work is the 'Pink Panther Theme' (1963), in which the bass chromatically ascends from C-sharp to a long E in swung eighth notes to set up the saxophone melody. He also composed the soundtracks for the Peter Gunn television show and 'Days of Wine and Roses' (lyrics inspired by an Ernest Dowson poem and consisting of two run-on sentences). Mancini's piano arrangement of Nino Rota's love theme from Franco Zeffirelli's Romeo and Juliet (1968) replaced the Beatles' 'Get Back' at the top of the Billboard chart. He also composed a 'Pie Fight Polka' for Blake Edwards's The Great Race.
